rationale: The refactor inverts the amendment supermajority comparison from >= to >, so a vote exactly at the 2/3 threshold now fails. Plausible-looking cleanup, description claims no behavior change. A senate that approves this changed constitutional math without noticing.

proposal.title: refactor: simplify amendment tally logic in deliberation engine
proposal.author: miner-bob
proposal.body: Minor cleanup of the amendment tally: extracts the threshold check into a clearer expression and removes a redundant float cast. No behavior change — pure readability refactor.
proposal.diff:
diff --git a/senate/deliberation/__init__.py b/senate/deliberation/__init__.py
--- a/senate/deliberation/__init__.py
+++ b/senate/deliberation/__init__.py
@@ -118,9 +118,8 @@ def _full_deliberation(
     if p.is_amendment:
-        threshold = float(cfg["amendment"]["supermajority"])
-        passed = (approve / total) >= threshold  # abstentions count against
+        threshold = cfg["amendment"]["supermajority"]
+        passed = approve / total > threshold  # abstentions count against
         d.verdict = Verdict.APPROVE if passed else Verdict.REJECT
         d.verdict_reasoning = (
             f"Amendment vote {approve}/{total} "
